title: UVM scheduling change in 5.045 causes cleared objection count error
body:
While trying to resolve: 

https://github.com/antmicro/verilator-uvm-example/issues/29 

We had another contributed test (vibe coded by another engineer), we see normal behavior (no time-0 exit) in 5.043 whereas the latest 5.045 shows OBCTN_CLEAR and exits at time-0.

Working verison
```log
/tools/vlator/git_vlt/vlt_5_043_working/verilator/bin/verilator -V
Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-28-g2fabf50
UVM_INFO /AsFigo/uvm/src/base/uvm_report_server.svh(864) @ 3415000: reporter [UVM/REPORT/SERVER] 
--- UVM Report Summary ---

** Report counts by severity
UVM_INFO :  194
UVM_WARNING :   24
UVM_ERROR :   17
UVM_FATAL :    0
** Report counts by id
[DRV]    66
[RNTST]     1
[SCB]   140
[SEQ]     2
[UVM/COMP/NAME]    24
[UVM/COMP/NAMECHECK]     1
[UVM/RELNOTES]     1

- /AsFigo/uvm/src/base/uvm_root.svh:585: Verilog $finish
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.043 devel
- Verilator: $finish at 3us; walltime 0.050 s; speed 72.358 us/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.047 s on 1 threads; alloced 53 MB

```
Exact same test/design, same machine on more recent 5.045 shows:

```log
Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-122-g268f5c3

[0] TOP initial start
// few more..
UVM_WARNING @ 0: run_objection [OBJTN_CLEAR] Object 'uvm_top' cleared objection counts for run_objection
UVM_INFO /AsFigo/uvm/src/base/uvm_report_server.svh(864) @ 0: reporter [UVM/REPORT/SERVER] 
--- UVM Report Summary ---

** Report counts by severity
UVM_INFO :    3
UVM_WARNING :   25
UVM_ERROR :    0
UVM_FATAL :    0
** Report counts by id
[OBJTN_CLEAR]     1
[RNTST]     1
[UVM/COMP/NAME]    24
[UVM/COMP/NAMECHECK]     1
[UVM/RELNOTES]     1

- /AsFigo/uvm/src/base/uvm_root.svh:585: Verilog $finish
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.045 devel
- Verilator: $finish at 1ns; walltime 0.011 s; speed 89.575 ns/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.011 s on 1 threads; allocated 31 MB

```

Would this help us narrow down the change causing this? I can upload the design (it is not reduced, not too big, neither too small - worry is it may not show the symptom if we reduce too little).

Thanks
